description.title: About Color Conversion
description.introP1: A color converter translates the same color across the formats used in web, print, and design — HEX for CSS, RGB/HSL for live tweaking, CMYK for print, LAB/OKLCH for perceptual uniformity.
description.spaceHex: HEX — compact 6/8-digit RGB notation. The default for CSS.
description.spaceRgb: RGB — additive primaries 0–255. Maps directly to display pixels.
description.spaceHsl: HSL — hue/saturation/lightness. Easier for humans to reason about than RGB.
description.spaceHsv: HSV — hue/saturation/value. Common in design tools but not a CSS function.
description.spaceCmyk: CMYK — subtractive primaries used in print. Approximate when converted from RGB.
description.spaceLab: LAB — perceptually uniform; equal numeric distance ≈ equal visual distance.
description.spaceOklch: OKLCH — modern perceptual space; preferred by Tailwind v4 and CSS Color Module Level 4.
description.harmoniesP1: Harmonies are mathematical relationships on the color wheel (complementary at 180°, triadic at 120°, etc.) that tend to produce balanced palettes.
description.contrastP1: WCAG 2.1 uses a luminance ratio (1:1 to 21:1) with thresholds for AA/AAA. It is widely supported but doesn't fully account for perception of small text or dark mode.
description.contrastP2: APCA is a candidate for WCAG 3 with a polarity-aware Lc score. It correlates better with readability for thin or small text.
description.visionP1: Roughly 1 in 12 men and 1 in 200 women have a form of color vision deficiency. The Vision toggle simulates protanopia, deuteranopia, tritanopia, and achromatopsia so you can verify your design holds up.
description.tipEyedropper: Use the eyedropper to pick a color from any pixel on your screen (Chromium browsers).
description.tipImage: Drop an image into the Palette tab to extract its 6 dominant colors via median-cut quantization.
description.tipOklch: Tailwind v4 emits colors in OKLCH for perceptual smoothness — copy the @theme block to align with that workflow.
